import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')

print("=== SHAPE ===")
print(df.shape)

print("\n=== DTYPES ===")
print(df.dtypes)

print("\n=== FIRST 5 ROWS ===")
print(df.head())

print("\n=== NULL COUNTS ===")
print(df.isnull().sum())

print("\n=== BASIC STATS ===")
print(df.describe().to_string())

print("\n=== UNIQUE VALUES PER COLUMN ===")
for col in df.columns:
    n = df[col].nunique()
    print(f"  {col}: {n} unique")

print("\n=== DUPLICATES ===")
print(f"Duplicate rows: {df.duplicated().sum()}")
print(f"Duplicate student_id: {df['student_id'].duplicated().sum()}")

print("\n=== VALUE COUNTS FOR DISCRETE COLUMNS ===")
for col in ['extracurriculars', 'absences']:
    print(f"\n{col}:")
    print(df[col].value_counts().sort_index())

print("\n=== CORRELATION MATRIX ===")
print(df.drop(columns='student_id').corr().round(3).to_string())

print("\n=== SKEWNESS ===")
print(df.drop(columns='student_id').skew().round(3))

print("\n=== KURTOSIS ===")
print(df.drop(columns='student_id').kurtosis().round(3))

# Check for potential outliers using IQR
print("\n=== OUTLIER CHECK (IQR method) ===")
for col in df.columns:
    if col == 'student_id':
        continue
    Q1, Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.25), df[col].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower, upper = Q1 - 1.5*IQR, Q3 + 1.5*IQR
    outliers = df[(df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper)]
    if len(outliers) > 0:
        print(f"  {col}: {len(outliers)} outliers (range [{lower:.1f}, {upper:.1f}], data range [{df[col].min()}, {df[col].max()}])")
    else:
        print(f"  {col}: no outliers")

.venv/bin/python3 << 'PYEOF'
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
features = ['weekly_study_hours', 'gpa', 'extracurriculars', 'commute_minutes', 'part_time_job_hours', 'absences']

# --- Plot 1: Distributions ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))
for idx, col in enumerate(features):
    ax = axes[idx // 3][idx % 3]
    ax.hist(df[col], bins=30, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7, color='steelblue')
    ax.set_title(f'{col}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel(col)
    ax.set_ylabel('Frequency')
    # add mean/median lines
    ax.axvline(df[col].mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean: {df[col].mean():.2f}')
    ax.axvline(df[col].median(), color='green', linestyle=':', label=f'Median: {df[col].median():.2f}')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.suptitle('Distribution of All Variables', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/01_distributions.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 1 saved")

# --- Plot 2: Correlation heatmap ---
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
corr = df[features].corr()
mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, dtype=bool), k=1)
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, fmt='.3f', cmap='RdBu_r', center=0, 
            vmin=-1, vmax=1, ax=ax, square=True)
ax.set_title('Correlation Matrix', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/02_correlation_heatmap.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 2 saved")

# --- Plot 3: Scatter matrix - features vs GPA ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 5, figsize=(20, 4))
predictors = [c for c in features if c != 'gpa']
for idx, col in enumerate(predictors):
    ax = axes[idx]
    ax.scatter(df[col], df['gpa'], alpha=0.3, s=10, color='steelblue')
    # Add LOWESS trend line
    from statsmodels.nonparametric.smoothers_lowess import lowess
    sorted_data = df[[col, 'gpa']].dropna().sort_values(col)
    smoothed = lowess(sorted_data['gpa'], sorted_data[col], frac=0.3)
    ax.plot(smoothed[:, 0], smoothed[:, 1], color='red', linewidth=2, label='LOWESS')
    ax.set_xlabel(col)
    ax.set_ylabel('GPA')
    ax.set_title(f'GPA vs {col}', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend()
fig.suptitle('GPA vs Each Predictor (with LOWESS trend)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/03_scatter_vs_gpa.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 3 saved")

# --- Plot 4: Box plots of GPA by categorical-like variables ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

# GPA by extracurriculars
df.boxplot(column='gpa', by='extracurriculars', ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title('GPA by Extracurriculars', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Number of Extracurriculars')
axes[0].set_ylabel('GPA')

# GPA by absences
df.boxplot(column='gpa', by='absences', ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title('GPA by Absences', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Number of Absences')
axes[1].set_ylabel('GPA')

plt.suptitle('')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/04_gpa_boxplots.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 4 saved")

# --- Plot 5: Pairplot (sampled for speed) ---
sample = df[features].sample(200, random_state=42)
g = sns.pairplot(sample, diag_kind='kde', plot_kws={'alpha': 0.3, 's': 15})
g.figure.suptitle('Pairplot (n=200 sample)', y=1.02, fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.savefig('plots/05_pairplot.png', dpi=100, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 5 saved")

print("\nAll initial plots saved.")
PYEOF

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from statsmodels.nonparametric.smoothers_lowess import lowess

df = pd.read_csv('dataset.csv')
features = ['weekly_study_hours', 'gpa', 'extracurriculars', 'commute_minutes', 'part_time_job_hours', 'absences']
predictors = [c for c in features if c != 'gpa']

# --- Plot 1: Distributions ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(15, 10))
for idx, col in enumerate(features):
    ax = axes[idx // 3][idx % 3]
    ax.hist(df[col], bins=30, edgecolor='black', alpha=0.7, color='steelblue')
    ax.set_title(f'{col}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.axvline(df[col].mean(), color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Mean: {df[col].mean():.2f}')
    ax.axvline(df[col].median(), color='green', linestyle=':', label=f'Median: {df[col].median():.2f}')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.suptitle('Distribution of All Variables', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/01_distributions.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 1 done")

# --- Plot 2: Correlation heatmap ---
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 8))
corr = df[features].corr()
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, fmt='.3f', cmap='RdBu_r', center=0, vmin=-1, vmax=1, ax=ax, square=True)
ax.set_title('Correlation Matrix', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/02_correlation_heatmap.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 2 done")

# --- Plot 3: Scatter vs GPA with LOWESS ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 5, figsize=(20, 4))
for idx, col in enumerate(predictors):
    ax = axes[idx]
    ax.scatter(df[col], df['gpa'], alpha=0.3, s=10, color='steelblue')
    sorted_data = df[[col, 'gpa']].sort_values(col)
    smoothed = lowess(sorted_data['gpa'].values, sorted_data[col].values, frac=0.3)
    ax.plot(smoothed[:, 0], smoothed[:, 1], color='red', linewidth=2, label='LOWESS')
    ax.set_xlabel(col)
    ax.set_ylabel('GPA')
    ax.set_title(f'GPA vs {col}', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.suptitle('GPA vs Each Predictor (with LOWESS trend)', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/03_scatter_vs_gpa.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 3 done")

# --- Plot 4: Boxplots ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
df.boxplot(column='gpa', by='extracurriculars', ax=axes[0])
axes[0].set_title('GPA by Extracurriculars', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Number of Extracurriculars')
axes[0].set_ylabel('GPA')
df.boxplot(column='gpa', by='absences', ax=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title('GPA by Absences', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Number of Absences')
axes[1].set_ylabel('GPA')
plt.suptitle('')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('plots/04_gpa_boxplots.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()
print("Plot 4 done")

print("All initial plots saved.")
PYEOF
